prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, nearly blank frame, with no clearly identifiable objects visible.

It then cuts to a simple black-and-white line drawing of a cartoon bird or hen on a plain light background. The character has a rounded body, thin legs, a beak, and appears to be wearing a head scarf or bonnet tied under the chin. One wing is raised, as if gesturing or waving. A pale, irregular shape is visible near the raised wing, but its exact identity is unclear from the frame.

Later, the visual switches to a product/title card on a dark background with large white lettering. The text reads “Best Food” or “Best Foods” at the top, “REAL” in the middle, and “Mayonnaise” below. In one sampled frame, part of the top text is cropped or partially obscured. In the next frame, the text is clearer: “Best Foods REAL Mayonnaise,” with a light splash-like or blot-like shape overlapping part of the top lettering.

By the final sampled frame, the ad shows a large product container or jar on the left, angled slightly. Its label reads “Best Foods” at the top, “REAL” on a banner, and “Mayonnaise” below; smaller text at the bottom appears to include “The BEST FOODS, Inc.” On the right side, the same cartoon hen character appears again in line drawing, standing beside the product. The background remains plain and light.

The sampled frames suggest a transition from animated character imagery to a product-name title card and finally to a combined product shot with the cartoon hen, but the intervening action between the widely spaced frames is not visible.
